steps.title: Quick question first…
steps.question: Which of these would increase the strength of an electromagnet?
options: Decreasing the current through the coil
Removing the iron core from the solenoid
Increasing the number of turns on the coil
Using a thicker wire but fewer turns
steps.misconceptionFeedback: ⚠️ Common misconception! Adding more turns to the coil increases the magnetic field strength — each loop contributes to the total field. Increasing current also strengthens the field, and adding a soft iron core concentrates the field lines enormously.
steps.correctFeedback: ✓ Correct! More turns on the coil means a stronger electromagnet. You can also increase current or add a soft iron core to boost the field strength.
steps.bestSource: BEST resource: AQA Physics specification 4.7.2 — The electromagnet

steps.title: Electromagnetism
steps.bodyHTML: A solenoid is a coil of wire that acts like a bar magnet when a current flows through it, producing a magnetic field along its axis. The field strength of an electromagnet can be increased by increasing the current, increasing the number of turns on the coil, or adding a soft iron core. Unlike permanent magnets, electromagnets can be switched on and off by controlling the current.

relationships: Soft iron core → much stronger field
No current → no field (can be switched off)

commonErrors: Thinking a thicker wire alone makes a stronger electromagnet
Confusing the number of turns with the length of wire used
Forgetting that the polarity depends on the direction of current

aqa.questionText: Describe three ways a student could increase the strength of an electromagnet.

steps.label: Method 1 — increase current
steps.content: Increase the current flowing through the wire in the coil.

steps.label: Method 2 — increase turns
steps.content: Increase the number of turns (loops) of wire in the coil.

steps.label: Method 3 — add iron core
steps.content: Place a soft iron core inside the solenoid to concentrate the magnetic field.
steps.markSchemeNote: 1 mark — add/use a soft iron core

aqa.finalAnswer: Increase current; increase number of turns; add a soft iron core.

aqa.questionText: A student wants to switch off an electromagnet instantly. What should they do?
markSchemeSteps: Electromagnets only work when current flows — switching off the current removes the field instantly ✓
aqa.commonMistake: Option B reverses polarity but does not switch the electromagnet off.
